content.text:
I think I see the problem. The SVG filter `#nav-item-warp` is defined inside an SVG element that has both `hidden` and `style="display: none"` (layout-main.webc:150-151). Safari has a known issue where it won't resolve `filter: url(#id)` references when the defining SVG is hidden with `display: none`. It effectively makes the filtered element invisible — which matches what you're seeing.

Let me check the full SVG wrapper.

result.output:
144→  <body>
   145→    <svg
   146→      xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
   147→      width="0"
   148→      height="0"
   149→      aria-hidden="true"
   150→      hidden
   151→      style="display: none"
   152→    >
   153→      <defs>
   154→        <filter id="nav-item-warp">
   155→          <feTurbulence
   156→            type="turbulence"
   157→            baseFrequency="0.06"
   158→            numOctaves="3"
   159→            seed="4"
   160→            result="turbulence"
   161→          />
   162→          <feDisplacementMap
   163→            in="SourceGraphic"
   164→            in2="turbulence"
   165→            scale="4"
   166→            xChannelSelector="R"
   167→            yChannelSelector="G"
   168→          />
   169→        </filter>
   170→      </defs>
   171→    </svg>
   172→
   173→    <page-header></page-header>

content.text:
That confirms it. The SVG on line 145-171 uses `hidden` and `style="display: none"` to hide the filter definitions. Safari (especially v26/WebKit) won't resolve `filter: url(#nav-item-warp)` from a `display: none` SVG, so the filtered `.nav-item-stroke` element becomes invisible.

The fix is to hide the SVG visually without using `display: none` or `hidden`. Replace those with a positional off-screen approach that keeps the SVG in the rendering tree:

- Remove `hidden` and `style="display: none"`
- Add `style="position: absolute; width: 0; height: 0; overflow: hidden"`

Want me to make that change?
oh, yeah remove the offending code and you can use the existing visually-hidden class (defined in tylergaw.css line 134)

input.old_string:
<svg
      xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
      width="0"
      height="0"
      aria-hidden="true"
      hidden
      style="display: none"
    >

input.description: Run prettier on the edited file

result.output:
Exit code 2
[error] No parser could be inferred for file "/Users/tylergaw/workspace/tylergaw.com/src/_includes/layout-main.webc".

content.text: Prettier doesn't have a parser for `.webc` files, but the edit is clean. Removed `width="0"`, `height="0"`, `hidden`, and `style="display: none"` and replaced them with `class="visually-hidden"`. That should keep the filter in the rendering tree so Safari can resolve the `url(#nav-item-warp)` reference.
check claude.md again, we're not doing prettier anymore on edit

Pull in the changes I've made in scheme-chooser. I want to have a hover effect that similar to what we're doing with nav-item-stroke, except I want it to happen on the svg cirlce, .button-stroke. I stubbed it out with the nav-item-warp filter, but can you make it actually animate. Feel free to create a filter/anything else directly in scheme-chooser since it's an outlier.

result.output: /Users/tylergaw/workspace/tylergaw.com/src/_components/scheme-chooser.webc

content.text: Now let me look at the nav-item-stroke to understand the animation pattern.

So the nav-item-stroke uses a squiggly animated path with an `feTurbulence`/`feDisplacementMap` filter for extra warp. For the circle button-stroke, I'll add an inline SVG filter with turbulence displacement and animate the seed so it has that jittery/squiggly feel on hover — similar vibe but self-contained in scheme-chooser.

input.old_string:
<svg
    xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
    width="24"
    height="24"
    viewBox="-1 -1 24 24"
  >
    <circle
      cx="11"
      cy="11"
      r="10"
      stroke="currentColor"
      stroke-width="1.2"
      fill="none"
      class="button-stroke"
    />
  </svg>
input.new_string:
<svg
    xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
    width="24"
    height="24"
    viewBox="-1 -1 24 24"
  >
    <defs>
      <filter id="button-stroke-warp">
        <feTurbulence
          type="turbulence"
          baseFrequency="0.06"
          numOctaves="3"
          seed="2"
          result="turbulence"
        >
          <animate
            attributeName="seed"
            values="2;8;3;10;5;12;7;2"
            dur="0.4s"
            calcMode="discrete"
            repeatCount="indefinite"
          />
        </feTurbulence>
        <feDisplacementMap
          in="SourceGraphic"
          in2="turbulence"
          scale="4"
          xChannelSelector="R"
          yChannelSelector="G"
        />
      </filter>
    </defs>
    <circle
      cx="11"
      cy="11"
      r="10"
      stroke="currentColor"
      stroke-width="1.2"
      fill="none"
      class="button-stroke"
    />
  </svg>

content.text:
Done. Here's what changed:

- Added a self-contained `<filter id="button-stroke-warp">` inside the button-stroke SVG with `feTurbulence` + `feDisplacementMap` (same approach as `nav-item-warp`)
- The filter animates its `seed` attribute across 8 values at `0.4s` with `calcMode="discrete"` — gives it that jittery squigglevision effect matching the nav strokes
- Updated the CSS to reference `#button-stroke-warp` instead of `#nav-item-warp`
lovely
Glad it's working!
